CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2023 Continued To Aug-23-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Jacob Lister, Esq., (919) 316-4255.

Jesse Valencia, Esq., ERSKINE LAW GROUP, APC, (949) 777-6032, for Defendant.

GENERAL MOTORS LLC's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Ruling - A R G U E D. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts the following tentative ruling: Defendant's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for concealment is overruled. The face of the complaint fails to show that the claim is time barred. (E-Fab, Inc. v. Accountants, Inc. Services (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1308, 1315.) The court takes plaintiff's allegation that he discovered defendant's wrongdoing "shortly before filing the complaint" as true. (Amended Complaint, pars. 7, 36.) The trier of fact will need to resolve whether the repair efforts of "Defendant's authorized repair facility" and representations that the vehicle was repaired estop defendant's reliance upon or tolls the statute of limitations. (Amended Complaint, pars. 32, 36.) Broberg v. Guardian Life Ins. Co. of America (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 912, 921 ["When a plaintiff reasonably should have discovered facts for purposes of the accrual of a cause of action is generally a question of fact"].)

Plaintiff alleges a sufficient relationship between plaintiff and defendant for the concealment claim. (Dhital v. Nissan N. Am. Inc. (2022) 84 Cal.App.5th 828, 844 [allegations that plaintiff purchased vehicle from Nissan dealership, that Nissan issued a warranty, and dealership was agent of Nissan were sufficient to show relationship with manufacturer for concealment claim] (review granted).)

Plaintiff's active concealment and exclusive knowledge allegations are sufficient to support the concealment cause of action. (Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651 [party may plead concealment claim based on active concealment or exclusive knowledge where no fiduciary relationship exists]; Amended Complaint, pars. 66-80.) The court notes that specificity requirement for a fraud/concealment claim is "relaxed when it is apparent from the allegations that the defendant necessarily possesses knowledge of the facts." (Quelimane Co. v. Steward Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 47; see also Alfaro v. Community Housing Imp. System & Planning Ass'n, Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.)

Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Defendant GENERAL MOTORS, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant's motion to strike is denied. The concealment cause of action supports the request for exemplary damages.
